compiled_state.title: As a project maintainer, I can rely on the CI llm-review job auto-dispatching security, performance, and test-quality overlays when the classifier flags them
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Extend ci-llm-review-runner.sh to auto-dispatch overlay agents (security, performance, test-quality) in parallel when the classifier flags fire.

**Why**: Local /dso:review auto-dispatches overlays based on classifier flags (security_overlay, performance_overlay, test_quality_overlay). Without this story in CI, overlay coverage is missing — security findings, performance regressions, and test-quality issues silently bypass the gate.

**Scope**:
- IN: overlay flag detection in classifier output; parallel dispatch of code-reviewer-{security-red-team, security-blue-team, performance, test-quality}.md; merge of overlay findings with tier findings into final reviewer-findings.json.
- OUT: tier dispatch (Story 1, 2); deliberate-violation test PR (Story 6).

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, when classifier output contains security_overlay=true, performance_overlay=true, or test_quality_overlay=true, the corresponding overlay agents are dispatched in parallel (alongside the tier dispatch, in the same parallel batch).
  ← Satisfies: "Overlay flags from classifier auto-dispatch corresponding overlay agents in parallel"
- When this story is complete, overlay agent findings are merged with tier findings into the final reviewer-findings.json (no overlay clobber, no tier clobber).
  ← Satisfies: "Overlay flags ... auto-dispatch corresponding overlay agents in parallel"
- unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Reliability] Security overlay is the only one with a red+blue team pair (red dispatched in parallel batch; blue dispatched serially after red returns). The other overlays (performance, test-quality) are single-agent dispatches.
- [Implicit-shared-state] Overlay findings must be written to slot-specific paths (reviewer-findings-security.json, reviewer-findings-performance.json, reviewer-findings-test-quality.json) and merged into the canonical reviewer-findings.json — never written directly to the canonical file in parallel with tier writes.

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
